english: domestics; ɣżi-byés (gzhi byes) Sch.: native and foreign, at home and abroad.

also ɣżu-mo (gzhu mo) Mil., resp. ɣnam-rú (gnam ru) B. and col., 1. bow, for shooting, ɣżu bcos (gzhu bcos) he constructed a bow Glr.; ɣżu géṅ-ba (gzhu geng ba), W. *káṅ-ce* (*kang ce*), to bend the bow and have it ready, frq.; _tén-pa ('then pa) Pth., and _gúgs-pa ('gugs pa) Cs., id.; _búd-pa ('bud pa) to unbend (the bow) Cs.; rdúṅ-ba (rdung ba) (Dzl. ༡༧, 15, ༡༨, 11. Gyatch. ༡༩, 10), acc. to explanations given by Lamas: to make the bow-string sound by a sudden pull or jerk, = ɣżu-rgyúd sbréṅ-ba (gzhu rgyud sbreng ba) Dzl., which both as to matter and language seems preferable to other explanations that have been given. — 2. arch, in architecture Cs., ɣżu-lugs-su _búb-pa (gzhu lugs su 'bub pa) 'to arch in the form of a bow' Cs.; capital, chapiter, v. ka-ba (ka ba). — 3. resp. for *żuṃ-már-pa* (*zhum mar pa*) lamp, *ziṃ-żu* (*zim zhu*) id., *góṅ-żu* (*gong zhu*) lantern C. (spelling uncertain). Comp. ɣżu-mkán (gzhu mkhan) bow-maker. — ɣżu-rgyúd (gzhu rgyud) bow-string Dzl. — ɣżú-can (gzhu can), ɣżu-ldán (gzhu ldan) furnished with a bow. — ɣżu-mčóg (gzhu mchog) Lex., Sch.: 'the two ends of a bow'; ɣżu-mčóg _dzúgs-pa (gzhu mchog 'dzugs pa) to rest one end of the bow on some object (?) Mil. — ɣżu-tóg (gzhu thog) an arched roof Cs. — ɣżu-tógs (gzhu thogs) holding a bow, archer Ld.-Glr. — ɣżu-brtán (gzhu brtan) n. of an ancient Indian king Gl. — ɣżu-dóms (gzhu doms) a cord, fathom, as a standard measure, opp. to any abitrary measure (so explained by a Lama). — ɣżú-pa (gzhu pa) bow-man, archer. — ɣżur-śúbs (gzhur shubs), bow-case Wdn.
to strike, to lash, lcág-gis (lcag gis) with a whip.

1. = mjúg (mjug), q. v., end, extremity; ɣżug-gu (gzhug gu), ɣżug-čuṅ (gzhug chung) Med. coccyx; rump or ventlet of birds Sch.; ɣżug-rméṅ (gzhug rmeng) the glands of it Sch.; gral-ɣżúg (gral gzhug) the end of a row Glr.; mgo-ɣżúg (mgo gzhug) upper and lower end, e.g. of a stick Glr.; lo-ɣżúg-la (lo gzhug la) at the end of the year Mil.; mṅag-ɣżúg (mngag gzhug) household-servants, suite Sch. — 2. v. _júg-pa ('jug pa).

1. the middle, midst. — 2. spinal marrow S.g., also klad-ɣżuṅ (klad gzhung) Sch., ɣżuṅ-ríṅs (gzhung rings) Mil. — *gyab-żuṅ-la zug rag* (*gyab zhung la zug rag*) W. I feel a pain in the middle of my back; lce-ɣżuṅ (lce gzhung) the middle of the tongue; ɣżuṅ-nas (gzhung nas) in a direct way, opp. to zúr-nas (zur nas). Comp. *żuṅ-go* (*zhung go*) C. middle door, principal door or gate. *żuṅ-cág* (*zhung cag*) W. partition-wall, *cád-ce* (*cad ce*) to construct one. — ɣżuṅ-pa (gzhung pa) a man from the middle part of the country, neither stód-pa (stod pa) nor śáṃ-pa (sham pa) W. — ɣżuṅ-ma (gzhung ma) 1. the middle of a thing Cs.; as a proper name: the middle part of Lhasa, containing the royal palace, also ɣżuṅ-sa-dga-ldán (gzhung sa dga' ldan). 2. the back-part of fur Sch. 3. kernel, pith, main substance Sch. 4. the original, the source, text; ɣżuṅ-lúgs (gzhung lugs) id. Tar.

pf. ɣżuṅs (gzhungs) Cs.: 'to attend, to be heedful; attention, ɣżúṅs-pa (gzhungs pa) heedful'; Sch. has: 'sincere, orderly', and for the current phrase yid ɣżúṅs-pa (yid gzhungs pa) he gives: 'a quiet and prudent mind or behaviour'. But the way in which the word is used in books, where it frequently occurs in conjunction with mkás-pa (mkhas pa), as well as in the popular expressions żuṅ-kán (zhung kan) and żuṅ-méd-kan (zhung med kan) = blo-rnó (blo rno) and blo-dmán (blo dman), would rather suggest the version: acuteness of perception, a good and quick comprehension.
Sch.: 'to go, to walk, to put into'.
excellent in its kind, gser ɣżún-po (gser gzhun po) the purest gold, ston-tóg ɣżún-po (ston thog gzhun po) a capital crop C.
to shear, shave, cut off, *ṭa* (*ṭa*) the hair C., leaves, branches Cs. (cf. bżár-ba (bzhar ba)?).

the act of remembering or reminding, *nyiṅ-la żen yóṅ-ce ńí-la ḍí* (*nying la zhen yong ce nyi la ḍi*) W. in order not to forget it, I have written it down; ɣżen skúl-ba (gzhen skul ba) Lex. to remind a person; ɣżen btád-pa (gzhen btad pa) or acc. to another reading btáb-pa (btab pa), i.e. _débs-pa ('debs pa) to admonish, exhort Dzl. ༡༧, 9.
to light, kindle, inflame Sch.; ráṅ-byuṅ-gi mes żúgs-la (rang byung gi mes zhugs la), prob. to be set in flames by spontaneous fire (?) Tar. 7, 4.
the second day after to-morrow Lex.; *to-re naṅ-la że-la* (*to re nang la zhe la*) W., to-
